mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.5 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the predictive Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of industrial production and retail sales drives equity returns; when INDPRO contracts while RSAFS expands, it indicates supply chain bottlenecks that compress profit margins and reduce forward S&P 500 performance.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change × RSAFS change) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression framework.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment, housing starts act as a leading indicator for consumer spending and construction-related corporate earnings, positively driving S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon when FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%.
failure_condition: HOUST growth fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns when FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, evidenced by a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a regime-filtered time-series regression.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: PAYEMS month-over-month changes show no monotonic relationship with 60-day forward SPX returns, or the sign of the predictive coefficient flips consistently across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index rises by more than 0.4% month-over-month, it signals accelerating inflation that forces expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity multiples and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL monthly changes above the threshold do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns in > 55% of observations, or the forward return differential between high and low CPI regimes is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising real personal savings and stable unemployment indicates strong consumer balance sheets that sustain discretionary spending, positively driving S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon when both series move in tandem.
failure_condition: The joint distribution of RSAFS growth and UNRATE stability fails to predict positive 5-day forward SPX returns, with an out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 in a multivariate predictive model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid economic contraction, industrial production becomes the dominant driver of equity valuations; a month-over-month decline exceeding 1.5% signals severe capacity utilization drops that depress S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines > 2.0% annualized in the latest quarter
failure_condition: INDPRO declines do not lead to negative 20-day SPX returns when GDPC1 is contracting, or the predictive power of INDPRO vanishes once GDP growth is controlled for in a multivariate regression.
